First this online system won't let me process fiscal years, so this is for 10/01/2022-9/30//2023. In late 2019 I received a phone call from the WA state Liquor Control Board telling me that the fundraiser that was done for our organization had to be run by the Non-Profit (me). We'd only receive a small amount from the fundraiser each year ($3K-$6K). So, I commenced to take on this tremendous task. I kept the books entirely separate along with separate bank accounts. The light bulb just came on that even though I kept the funds separate, I needed to report it all as one. So, because in 2019 to2020 (my FY 2020) it became my first year I made over the $50K limit to submit the e-postcard N990. So I am amending 2020, 2022, 2023 & 2024 on a N990-EZ's. In 2021 due to COVID all my events were canceled.
The organization was formed in 2024 and was unaware of the annual Form 990-EZ filing requirement Upon discovery the organization filed promptly

The prior return, prepared by the FL Center for Economic Initia was incomplete and incorrect when it was submitted, and the errors were discovered by one of our board members in December 2025. This is the amended return with the correct revenue and expense amounts.

The Beta Xi Chapter filed an extension (automatic 6 months); consequently, the extended due date for the TY24 (6/30/25) is 5/15/26. Therefore, the Form 990 is being filed timely.

The late filing was due to an administrative transition and capacity constraints during a period of organizational growth. During the reporting year, the organization was managing a large grant award while simultaneously strengthening internal financial controls and preparing records for compliance. The delay was unintentional, corrective steps have been taken to ensure timely filings going forward, and the organization is now fully current with all reporting requirements.
